commit_msg: docs: write codeStory README with full project spec
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Confession Written in Markdown
subtitle: Sixty seconds later, he typed the whole truth into a README.
when_where: 🕐 9:05 AM, same morning. Thirty seconds have passed. Main branch hasn't moved, but Omkar's fingers are already flying. 📝 The README is where the game changes—not a technical specification, but a *manifesto*. He's writing down everything codeStory is meant to do, meant to be. The sun is higher now. He's sweating.
who_whom: 🔍 The README becomes the victim and the weapon both. Omkar is writing to an audience he doesn't know exists yet—future developers, maybe himself reading this later, trying to understand what possessed him. 📖 He's documenting the full project spec like a man recording his final words before the fall. Every line is a confession. Every bullet point is evidence.
what_why: ✍️ In sixty seconds, he's laid bare the entire vision: what codeStory does, why it exists, how it will work. 🎯 There's no hiding now. The README is public. Anyone reading it will understand his obsession—to turn the invisible work of programming into a *narrative*, to make the code *speak*. 🧠 But what he's really documented is his own need to be seen, to have his work witnessed and judged. 💀 The confession is written. The trial can begin.
verdict: He didn't write a README; he wrote a suicide note for his sanity.
